summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard transfer showcases a stellar show highlighted by the soaring He's Gone → Other One suite and a tight Miracle‑Bertha‑Good Lovin' sequence.
review: The Miller SBD transfer is crisp, balanced and noticeably upgraded over previous sources, delivering an exceptionally clear listening experience. Musically the concert shines with a unique He's Gone > Other One jam, a brilliant Miracle‑Bertha‑Good Lovin' run and standout moments like Big River’s physics‑defying solo, though the second set has a few uneven spots (out‑of‑tune jams and a lackluster Truckin'). Overall fans regard this as one of the better ’79 recordings.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both performance and audio quality across 7 of 8 reviews; only a single mixed‑negative review lowers the average slightly.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with full band balance; upgraded transfer and drum patches remove previous flaws, providing a clean, listenable SBD.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds nicely with strong openings (Shakedown, Big River) and solid mid‑set work; second set delivers a soaring He's Gone > Other One jam but includes some uneven, lifeless moments and tuning issues.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for “different planet” playing, physics‑defying solo on Big River and outstanding tone throughout the show.
